sections.title: Use
rows: To talk about imaginary past situations — things that did not happen — and to say what the result would have been
If I **had remembered** her birthday, she **would have been** happier.
If they **hadn't missed** the flight, they **would have arrived** on time.
If we **had brought** more food, everyone **would have eaten** enough.
If he **had studied** harder, he **wouldn't have failed** the course.

sections.title: Useful notes
items: If you had trained more, you **might have won** the race. (uncertain outcome)
If she had borrowed my laptop, she **could have finished** the project earlier. (ability in the past)

sections.title: Be careful
items: The third conditional always refers to the past: **If I had brought my charger, my phone wouldn't have died.**
The second conditional uses past simple but refers to the present or future: **If I had more free time, I would travel more.**
Second conditional form: **if + past simple, would + bare infinitive** — used for imaginary or unlikely situations in the present or future: **If she lived nearby, we would meet more often.**

exercises.exerciseTypeName: A: Complete the third conditional sentences
exercises.title: A: Complete the third conditional sentences
exercises.instructions: Use the correct form of the verbs in brackets.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the correct form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: If I ...... (remember) his birthday, I ...... (send) him a card.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is third conditional, so the correct answer is "had remembered, would have sent".

wrongOptions.remembered, would have sent: Without had, the earlier-past relationship is lost.
wrongOptions.remember: The bare infinitive (remember) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple only shows one past event — use had + past participle for the earlier action.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Name the conditional type first — that tells you which verb forms belong in each clause.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Third conditional: if + past perfect, would have + past participle — an unreal past result.

items.sentence: If they ...... (book) earlier, they ...... (find) cheaper tickets.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is third conditional, so the correct answer is "had booked, would have found".

wrongOptions.booked, would have found: Without had, the earlier-past relationship is lost.
wrongOptions.book: The bare infinitive (book) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple only shows one past event — use had + past participle for the earlier action.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Name the conditional type first — that tells you which verb forms belong in each clause.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Third conditional: if + past perfect, would have + past participle — an unreal past result.

items.sentence: If we ...... (not forget) the camera, we ...... (take) more photos.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is third conditional, so the correct answer is "hadn't forgotten, would have taken".

wrongOptions.not forget: The bare infinitive (not forget) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple only shows one past event — use had + past participle for the earlier action.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Name the conditional type first — that tells you which verb forms belong in each clause.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Third conditional: if + past perfect, would have + past participle — an unreal past result.

items.sentence: If she ...... (listen) to the directions, she ...... (not get) lost.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is third conditional, so the correct answer is "had listened, wouldn't have got".

wrongOptions.listened, wouldn't have got: Without had, the earlier-past relationship is lost.
wrongOptions.listen: The bare infinitive (listen) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple only shows one past event — use had + past participle for the earlier action.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Name the conditional type first — that tells you which verb forms belong in each clause.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Third conditional: if + past perfect, would have + past participle — an unreal past result.

items.sentence: If he ...... (wear) warmer clothes, he ...... (not catch) a cold.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is third conditional, so the correct answer is "had worn, wouldn't have caught".

wrongOptions.worn, wouldn't have caught: Without had, the earlier-past relationship is lost.
wrongOptions.wear: The bare infinitive (wear) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple only shows one past event — use had + past participle for the earlier action.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Name the conditional type first — that tells you which verb forms belong in each clause.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Third conditional: if + past perfect, would have + past participle — an unreal past result.

items.sentence: If you ...... (save) your work, you ...... (not lose) everything.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is third conditional, so the correct answer is "had saved, wouldn't have lost".

wrongOptions.saved, wouldn't have lost: Without had, the earlier-past relationship is lost.
wrongOptions.save: The bare infinitive (save) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple only shows one past event — use had + past participle for the earlier action.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Time words like already, yet, since, and for are strong signals for present perfect.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Third conditional: if + past perfect, would have + past participle — an unreal past result.

exercises.exerciseTypeName: B: Choose would have, could have or might have
exercises.title: B: Choose would have, could have or might have
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the best option.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each sentence with the best option.

items.sentence: If I had seen your message earlier, I ...... replied immediately.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is certain result, so the correct answer is "would have".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "would have", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Conditional sentences pair specific verb forms in the if-clause and main clause.

items.sentence: If we had brought a map, we ...... avoided getting lost.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is possible ability, so the correct answer is "could have".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "could have", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Conditional sentences pair specific verb forms in the if-clause and main clause.

items.sentence: If she had gone to the doctor sooner, she ...... recovered faster.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is uncertain result, so the correct answer is "might have".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "might have", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Conditional sentences pair specific verb forms in the if-clause and main clause.

items.sentence: If they had left before rush hour, they ...... arrived on time.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is certain result, so the correct answer is "would have".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple only shows one past event — use past perfect when one past action happened before another.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Past perfect describes an action that happened before another past action.

items.sentence: If I had borrowed your notes, I ...... studied more effectively.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is possible ability, so the correct answer is "could have".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "could have", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Conditional sentences pair specific verb forms in the if-clause and main clause.

items.sentence: If he had accepted the invitation, he ...... enjoyed the party.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is uncertain result, so the correct answer is "might have".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "might have", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Conditional sentences pair specific verb forms in the if-clause and main clause.

exercises.title: C: Error correction
exercises.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct form.
exercises.studentInstruction: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct form.

items.displayPrompt: If I would had more time, I would have helped you.

cues: If I would had more time, I would have helped you.

items.answer: If I had had more time, I would have helped you.
acceptedAnswers: If I had had more time, I would have helped you.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Past perfect.
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: If I had had more time, I would have helped you.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Past perfect shows the earlier of two past actions — use had + past participle before the later past event.

wrongOptions.Past: Check how "Past" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.
wrongOptions.perfect: Check how "perfect" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Match the tense shift shown in the explanation — one verb often moves back one step in time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Write one complete sentence with correct punctuation — capital letter at the start and a full stop at the end.
explanationContent.question: If I <mistake>would</mistake> had more time, I would have helped you.
explanationContent.correctedSentence: If I had had more time, I would have helped you.

items.displayPrompt: If she had drank less coffee, she would have slept better.

cues: If she had drank less coffee, she would have slept better.

items.answer: If she had drunk less coffee, she would have slept better.
acceptedAnswers: If she had drunk less coffee, she would have slept better.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Past participle.
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: If she had drunk less coffee, she would have slept better.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Reported speech backshifts tenses and time/place words (now → then, tomorrow → the next day, here → there).

wrongOptions.Past: Check how "Past" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.
wrongOptions.participle: Check how "participle" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Match the tense shift shown in the explanation — one verb often moves back one step in time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Write one complete sentence with correct punctuation — capital letter at the start and a full stop at the end.
explanationContent.question: If she had <mistake>drank</mistake> less coffee, she would have slept better.
explanationContent.correctedSentence: If she had drunk less coffee, she would have slept better.

items.displayPrompt: If we had took a taxi, we would have arrived earlier.

cues: If we had took a taxi, we would have arrived earlier.

items.answer: If we had taken a taxi, we would have arrived earlier.
acceptedAnswers: If we had taken a taxi, we would have arrived earlier.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Past participle.
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: If we had taken a taxi, we would have arrived earlier.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Reported speech backshifts tenses and time/place words (now → then, tomorrow → the next day, here → there).

wrongOptions.Past: Check how "Past" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.
wrongOptions.participle: Check how "participle" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Match the tense shift shown in the explanation — one verb often moves back one step in time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Write one complete sentence with correct punctuation — capital letter at the start and a full stop at the end.
explanationContent.question: If we had <mistake>took</mistake> a taxi, we would have arrived earlier.
explanationContent.correctedSentence: If we had taken a taxi, we would have arrived earlier.

wrongOptions.Past: Check how "Past" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.
wrongOptions.participle: Check how "participle" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.

exercises.exerciseTypeName: D: Rewrite with the third conditional
exercises.title: D: Rewrite with the third conditional
exercises.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
I forgot my passport, so I couldn't board the plane. **IF**
If I ...... my passport, I could have boarded the plane.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is forgot passport, so the correct answer is "hadn't forgotten".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "hadn't forgotten", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
She didn't charge her phone, so it turned off. **HAD**
If she ...... her phone, it wouldn't have turned off.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is didn't charge, so the correct answer is "had charged".

wrongOptions.charged: Without had, the earlier-past relationship is lost — use past perfect for the first of two past actions.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "had charged", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "had charged" is a phrasal verb or fixed chunk that collocates with the rest of the sentence.

items.sentence:
We didn't reserve a table, so we didn't get a seat. **WOULD**
If we had reserved a table, we ...... a seat.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is no reservation, so the correct answer is "would have got".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "would have got", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Conditional sentences pair specific verb forms in the if-clause and main clause.

items.sentence:
Tom didn't revise enough, so he failed the test. **COULD**
If Tom had revised more, he ...... the test.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is failed test, so the correct answer is "could have passed".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "could have passed", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "could have passed" is a phrasal verb or fixed chunk that collocates with the rest of the sentence.

items.sentence:
It snowed heavily, so the match was cancelled. **PLAYED**
If it hadn't snowed heavily, they ...... the match.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is match cancelled, so the correct answer is "would have played".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "would have played", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Passive voice focuses on the action or object, not who performs it.

items.sentence:
I stayed up too late, so I felt exhausted. **FELT**
If I hadn't stayed up so late, I ...... exhausted.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is stayed up late, so the correct answer is "wouldn't have felt".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "wouldn't have felt", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.
